mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60 days (p > 0.05), or the interaction term loses explanatory power in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between INDPRO growth and 20-day returns reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during recovery periods, with Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or flat 20-day forward return distribution, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative alpha in a long-short portfolio test.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, simultaneous declines in industrial production and rising inflation indicate stagflationary pressures that erode corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The stagflation signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect lacks statistical significance.
